panels.title: Redis Connection Create P95 by Service

options.content:
• Use the dashboard header link or open VictoriaTraces in Explore for span search.
  • Current traced services typically include gorilla, gorilla-executor, gorilla-filemeta, gorilla-flat-run-fields-updater, gorilla-glue, gorilla-metric-observer, and gorilla-parquet.
  • The HTTP panels in this dashboard are keyed off service_name="gorilla"; deeper workload breakdown is best seen in traces and DB/pool telemetry until more services publish first-class HTTP metrics.

panels.title: Telemetry Self-Health (can I trust this data?)

options.content:
### Read this FIRST when a dashboard looks wrong

**What it shows** — the fraction of trace spans the collector failed to export to storage. If this is non-zero, the trace/RED panels across every dashboard are **undercounting** — the data itself is lossy, not the app.

**What bad looks like** — sustained > ~1%. Above ~5% treat trace-derived numbers as unreliable.

**Do next** — if failures are high, check the `victoria-otlp-gateway` collector pod and the VictoriaTraces backend before trusting any trace panel. Also cross-check **Scrape Success** / **Down Targets** at the top of this dashboard for metric-side gaps.

panels.title: Trace ingestion by receiver (DataDog / OTLP)
panels.description: Spans/sec the collector accepts, split by receiver. The `datadog` receiver (port 8126) is the DataDog-APM path — background/SDK services (weave-trace, glue, parquet, filemeta, metric-observer, anaconda2) send here; `otlp` is the main gorilla trace path.

options.content:
**What it shows** — whether each ingestion path on the gateway is receiving data. This is how you confirm **DataDog** traces/metrics are populated.

- **`datadog`** — DataDog-APM traces (port 8126). A non-zero line here means DataDog-protocol tracing is flowing; downstream it appears as `traces_spanmetrics_calls_total` for the SDK services.
- **`otlp`** — the main gorilla OpenTelemetry path (the bulk of spans/metrics).
- **`statsd`** — DogStatsD metrics (port 8125). **Expect this to be absent/zero** unless gorilla runs with `GORILLA_STATSD_PORT` > 0 (a core/manifest-side setting). Zero here is the reason the DogStatsD-only panels were dropped.

**What bad looks like** — a receiver you expect traffic on flatlining at 0, or `otelcol_receiver_refused_*` climbing (data arriving but rejected).

panels.title: Metric-point ingestion by receiver (DogStatsD / OTLP)
panels.description: Metric points/sec the collector accepts, split by receiver. A `statsd` line appears only when gorilla emits DogStatsD (GORILLA_STATSD_PORT > 0); today only `otlp` carries metrics.

options.content:
**Pairs with the panel to the left.** If a receiver shows accepted traffic but data still isn't landing in dashboards, check the refused counters:

- `sum by (receiver) (rate(otelcol_receiver_refused_spans[$__rate_interval]))`
- `sum by (receiver) (rate(otelcol_receiver_refused_metric_points[$__rate_interval]))`

**Accepted > 0, refused ≈ 0** = healthy ingestion. **Refused climbing** = the gateway is receiving but rejecting (bad payloads, queue full) — check the `victoria-otlp-gateway` collector logs.
